title: New languages added
summary: Frames expands language support with Czech, Slovak, Hungarian, Croatian, and Malay, making the app feel more natural worldwide. Free accounts can now add up to 5 rolls to try the app comfortably.

sections.alt: Frames app is now available in Czech
sections.caption: Frames iOS 1.27 update

sections.content: Frames is now available in Czech, Slovak, Hungarian, Croatian, and Malay, adding to the existing set of supported languages: English, Arabic, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hebrew, Hindi, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian Bokmål, Polish, Portuguese, Romanian, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Spanish, Swedish, Thai, Traditional Chinese, Turkish, Ukrainian, and Vietnamese.
This expansion is part of making Frames more accessible to users around the world, so the app feels natural to use no matter the language, and fits more closely with how people work day to day.
Free accounts can now create up to 5 rolls, increased from 3. This gives more room to try the app and organize a few shoots before deciding to upgrade.
The goal is to keep Frames open and easy to try, so anyone can explore it at their own pace before choosing a plan.

improvements: Added Czech, Slovak, Hungarian, Croatian, and Malay localization
Increased free tier limit to 5 rolls
meta_description: Frames adds Czech, Slovak, Hungarian, Croatian, and Malay support, and increases the free limit to 5 rolls.
